rationale: Builds on the current soft quant-aware projection winner, but pivots away from more quantizer churn. The default run only optimizes `final_int8_zlib_roundtrip_exact`, yet the script still spends wallclock on a last-step full-precision validation pass and also keeps a conservative 72s reserve floor that dominates the estimate-based budget. This version removes that default end-of-train float eval unless `VAL_LOSS_EVERY` is explicitly enabled, and lowers the reserve floor so the existing measured final-eval estimate can reclaim otherwise idle time for extra training steps without touching the required final int8 roundtrip path.
title: Reserve Reclaim By Skipping Default Final Float Eval
